geographic_restrictions: Must be based within CET +/- 3 timezones
experience_requirements: 5+ years production TypeScript or JavaScript experience
Production experience with a major frontend framework such as React, Vue, Svelte, or similar
Hands-on Web3 frontend experience with wagmi, viem, ethers.js, or equivalents
Experience integrating multiple wallets such as MetaMask, WalletConnect, or hardware wallets
Solidity reading proficiency and ability to write minor contracts when needed
Frontend component and integration testing experience
preferred_skills: open-source contributions to wagmi, viem, ethers, Foundry, or similar
key_responsibilities: Own Arkiv's flagship Bridge UI for L2 to L3 token transfers
Implement wallet connect, network switching, signing, status tracking, and edge-case handling
Debug failed transactions with calldata inspection and tools such as Tenderly
Write minor Solidity contracts in Foundry for faucets and helpers when needed
Pair with Senior Solidity Engineer on bridge and account abstraction flows
Set quality standards for Web3 UX, accessibility, resilience, and user-facing code review
Build client-side telemetry for wallet errors, RPC failures, and transaction success rates
Back up developer onboarding, docs, faucet, sample apps, homepage, and L3 explorer operations
Collaborate with Platform, Protocol, SRE, and DevRel teams
success_outcomes: Ship a production-grade Bridge UI resilient to wallet quirks, RPC failures, and stuck transactions
Improve smart-contract integration quality across Arkiv user-facing surfaces
Make Web3 UX clearer, more accessible, and less brittle
Expose client-side observability that reflects real user transaction outcomes
Reduce single points of failure across user-facing Arkiv surfaces
Support mainnet launch readiness for Arkiv
must_have_checklist: Must be based within CET +/- 3 timezones
Own or ship resilient dApp surfaces such as a Bridge UI
Web3 frontend library experience with wagmi, viem, ethers.js, or equivalent
Minor Solidity contract writing in Foundry
ABI integration and failed transaction debugging
Gas, nonce, and replacement transaction understanding
Client-side observability for wallet errors, RPC failures, and transaction outcomes
Cross-functional collaboration with protocol, platform, SRE, and DevRel
red_flags: Solidity reading proficiency is described as non-negotiable
The role centers on Bridge UI and wallet edge cases, so missing wallet-integration evidence is high risk
Location is limited to CET +/- 3 timezones
Foundry and minor Solidity contract writing are explicit requirements
Prior bridge UI or cross-chain dApp experience is a strong plus and likely a high-signal differentiator
